title: Rakririan

{{Quote|Buy? Buy! Oh, come, sir, and see-see-see! Any pet worth having is to be had at Sabodor's, best in the Sector.|Sabodor|<ref name="HSASE" />}}
The '''Rakririans''' were [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Arthropod/Legends|arthropods]] indigenous to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Rakrir]]. The [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]] were distinctive in appearance, with five pairs of [[Limb/Legends|limbs]] and [[Ommatophora|stalk-mounted]] [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. They rarely left their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], finding the wider [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] too unrefined for their sophisticated tastes. Notable Rakririans that bucked that trend were the [[athlete]] [[Mogoyu]], the fencer of stolen goods [[Endrigorn]], and the exotic pet-store owner [[Sabodor]].

==Biology and appearance==
Rakririans were an [[Insectoid/Legends|insectoid]], [[Arthropod/Legends|arthropod]] [[Species/Legends|species]] with tubular bodies, divided into many segments. Adult bodies ranged in length from 1.8 to 3.6 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] long, with five sets of [[Limb/Legends|limbs]] spaced along their central body segment.<ref name="AE" /> While they typically stood upright on the last two sets of limbs,<ref name="GSW">''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe]]''</ref> they were able to run on all five sets if necessary.<ref name="HNN52">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|52|sports/13418_1.html|New Galaxy's Fastest Hexaped}}</ref> Atop their body was a pair of [[Ommatophora|eyestalks]] that moved incessantly. They had a tiny [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]] and notable olfactory cluster. A cantilevered vocal organ was located in the middle of their midsection.<ref name="AE" /> Rakririans had three [[Gender/Legends|sexes]]: males, females and facilitator drones.<ref name="CN">''[[Coruscant Nights I: Jedi Twilight]]''</ref>

==Society and culture==
The Rakririan [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] was the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Rakrir]]<ref name="HSASE" /><ref name="HNN52" />—sometimes alternatively known as Ballikite<ref name="AE" />—which was located in the [[Kwymar sector/Legends|Kwymar sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> Rakririans developed into highly refined and cultured beings, seen by some as excessively finicky and meticulous. As a result of that, few ever left their homeworld, failing to find sufficiently sophisticated locations in the wider galaxy. Those that did, tended to either be extremely wealthy, or work for the extremely wealthy in service industries.<ref name="AE" /> Rakririans did not wear any clothes, although some wore [[Bracelet/Legends|bracelets]] for personal ornamentation.<ref name="image">Based on images in ''[[Alien Encounters]]'' and ''[[Han Solo and the Corporate Sector Sourcebook]]''</ref>

Non-Rakririan found it difficult to read their body language, although certain movements did convey their frame of mind. For instance, slow opening and closing of their mandibles could mean either that they were in a receptive frame of mind, or, alternatively, that they were preparing to defend themselves.<ref name="CN" /> Although they could speak [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]], it was often with a strong buzzing accent that some found difficult to understand.<ref name="CN" />

==Rakririans in the galaxy==
[[File:Sabodar.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Sabodor, a Rakririan pet-shop owner.]]
While few Rakririans ventured off their homeworld,<ref name="Han Solo and the Corporate Sector Sourcebook">''[[Han Solo and the Corporate Sector Sourcebook]]''</ref> there were a few notable exceptions. A famous Rakririan [[athlete]] was [[Mogoyu]], who finished second—in a [[Holocamera/Legends|holocamera]] finish—in the "decaped-plus" category of the [[Mobquet Presents: Fastest Land Beings]] event held on [[Teyr/Legends|Teyr]] in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]].<ref name="HNN52" /> A few [[Standard year/Legends|years]] later, [[Endrigorn]], a Rakririan facilitator drone, worked as a fence in the [[Coruscant Underworld/Legends|lower levels]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. There, it dealt primarily in stolen light sculptures, [[Holographic art|holo-art]], and precious gems. It bought a [[hypergem]] off [[Kaird]], a [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] [[Vigo/Legends|Vigo]], who hoped Endrigorn would pass it on to his rival [[Xizor/Legends|Xizor]].<ref name="CN" />

Another well-known Rakririan who left his homeworld was [[Sabodor]], who set up [[Sabodor's/Legends|Sabodor's]], an exotic pet-shop on [[Etti IV/Legends|Etti IV]]. One of the numerous pet-shops that catered to the whims of Etti's wealthy, Sabodor's was considered the best. There, he provided the wealthy with unique pets to flaunt their wealth. Sabodor once sold a [[Dinko (creature)|dinko]] to [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], who gave it to [[Ploovo Two-For-One/Legends|Ploovo Two-For-One]] as a "gift."<ref name="HSASE" />

A common Rakririan export to the galaxy was their [[Rakririan Burnout Sauce|Burnout Sauce]]. Extremely [[Spice/Legends|spicy]], most sentients used it only in trace quantities to avoid potential side effects, such as burns to the mouth and throat.<ref name="SWG">{{GalaxiesAED}}</ref> The [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Plo Koon/Legends|Plo Koon]] was known to use Burnout Sauce.<ref name="JC">{{Tales|7|Jedi Chef}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
[[Brian Daley]] created Rakririans for his novel, ''[[Han Solo at Stars' End]]'', published [[April]] [[1979]]. They were later included in the ''[[Han Solo and the Corporate Sector Sourcebook]]''. While both these sources established the Rakririan homeworld as Rakrir, ''[[Alien Encounters]]'' later claimed it was Ballikite. Rakririans have subsequently appeared in ''[[HoloNet News Vol. 531 52]]'' and ''[[Coruscant Nights I: Jedi Twilight]]''.
